summary: Iteration 44 - SNMPv3 Feature Testing Complete. Backend: 12/12 tests passed (100%). All SNMP v3 API endpoints working correctly. Frontend: SnmpConfigPanel component exists with proper data-testid attributes but is only rendered for discovered endpoints, not for regular managed devices in the detail panel.

integration_issues.issue: SnmpConfigPanel is only rendered in EndpointInfo component (for discovered endpoints). It's not shown in the main DeviceDetailPanel for regular managed devices. The condition `detail?.managed && clientId && detail?.device_id` in EndpointInfo function limits visibility.
integration_issues.priority: MEDIUM - Component exists but not accessible for all device types

backend_api: PUT /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices/{device_id}/snmp - Update to v3 with full USM credentials (200 OK)
PUT /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices/{device_id}/snmp - Update to v3 with authNoPriv security level (200 OK)
PUT /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices/{device_id}/snmp - Revert to v2c clears v3 fields (200 OK)
GET /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices - Returns devices with snmp_version field
GET /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices - Requires auth or API key
POST /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices - Create device with SNMPv3 fields
POST /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices - Create device with v2c default
All SNMPv3 USM fields accepted: snmpv3_username, snmpv3_auth_protocol, snmpv3_auth_password, snmpv3_priv_protocol, snmpv3_priv_password, snmpv3_security_level
Device reverted to v2c after testing (cleanup)
frontend_ui: Version selector has data-testid='snmp-version-select' with v1/v2c/v3 options
Community string input has data-testid='snmp-community-input' (shown for v1/v2c)
SNMPv3 USM fields have proper data-testid attributes: snmpv3-security-level, snmpv3-username-input, snmpv3-auth-proto, snmpv3-auth-password, snmpv3-priv-proto, snmpv3-priv-password
Component conditionally shows v3 fields when v3 is selected
Component conditionally shows auth/priv fields based on security level

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add SnmpConfigPanel to main DeviceDetailPanel content section (not just EndpointInfo) so it's visible for all managed devices, not just discovered endpoints
critical_code_review_comments: models.py: ManagedDevice model correctly includes all SNMPv3 fields (snmp_version, snmpv3_username, snmpv3_auth_protocol, snmpv3_auth_password, snmpv3_priv_protocol, snmpv3_priv_password, snmpv3_security_level)
connector.py: PUT /snmp endpoint correctly handles v1/v2c/v3 switching, clears v3 fields when reverting to v1/v2c
connector.py: POST managed-devices correctly stores all SNMPv3 fields on device creation
SnmpConfigPanel.js: Well-structured component with proper state management and API integration
DeviceDetailPanel.js: SnmpConfigPanel only rendered in EndpointInfo function - may want to also show for regular managed devices

success_rate.frontend: Component verified but not fully testable via UI (panel not visible for regular devices)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 44 tested SNMPv3 feature. Backend APIs fully working: PUT /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices/{device_id}/snmp for updating SNMP config (v1/v2c/v3), GET /api/connector/{client_id}/managed-devices returns devices with snmp_version and snmpv3_* fields, POST creates devices with v3 config. Frontend SnmpConfigPanel component exists with all required data-testid attributes but is only rendered for discovered endpoints in DeviceDetailPanel. The test device was reverted to v2c after testing.

mocked_apis.note: All SNMPv3 APIs are real implementations

snmp_update_endpoint.status: WORKING - updates snmp_version and USM credentials

managed_devices_list_endpoint.status: WORKING - returns devices with snmp_version, snmpv3_* fields

managed_device_create_endpoint.status: WORKING - creates device with SNMPv3 configuration

managed_device_model.status: WORKING - all fields accepted and persisted

frontend_snmp_config_panel.status: COMPONENT EXISTS - but only visible for discovered endpoints, not regular managed devices
